message:
test(ftui-extras): improve stopwatch.rs test coverage (bd-1gmqr)

Add 34 new tests (28→62 total) covering builder/accessor methods,
start/stop/toggle idempotency, reset while stopped, multi-cycle
accumulation, tick_once with custom intervals, tick return values,
view after stop/reset, compact format edge cases (exact second,
fractional µs/ms, exact units, nano precision, large hours),
clock format boundaries (exact minute/hour, large hours, max
minutes/seconds, single second), zero interval, sub-ms interval,
and derive trait coverage for DisplayFormat and Stopwatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1fyc5","title":"State: command enum + reducer","description":"Goal
- Create a command/message enum and a reducer that updates state deterministically.

Deliverables
- Command variants for all keybindings and UI actions.
- Reducer returns explicit re-render intent.

Acceptance
- Commands are testable and free of side effects.

@@ -1320,7 +1321,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-4r5h","title":"Text Effects: Preset Library","description":"## Background
Users want great results without understanding individual effects. Presets provide curated, ready-to-use combinations that showcase the system's capabilities.

## Implementation

Add TextPreset enum:
```rust
pub enum TextPreset {
    // High-energy/modern
    Neon,       // Cyan/magenta wave, pulsing glow
    Cyberpunk,  // Glitch, chromatic aberration, animated gradient
    Matrix,     // Green cascade, fade trail, cyber chars
    
    // Classic/retro
    Retro,      // CRT scanlines, green phosphor, subtle flicker
    Typewriter, // Reveal by char with cursor block
    Terminal,   // Solid green, subtle pulse, no frills
    
    // Elegant/professional
    Cinematic,  // Slow fade, gold gradient, soft glow
    Elegant,    // Organic pulse, gold/cream gradient, drop shadow
    Minimal,    // Subtle fade, no color, clean
    
    // Dynamic/playful
    Fire,       // Fire gradient, upward wave motion
    Ice,        // Frost blue gradient, sparkle effect
    Hologram,   // Rainbow shift, scanlines, transparency pulse
}
```

### Preset Specifications

| Preset | Effects | Colors | Special |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| Neon | Wave, PulsingGlow, ColorWave | Cyan/Magenta | Bold text |
| Cyberpunk | Glitch, ChromaticAberration, AnimatedGradient | Cyberpunk palette | Occasional matrix chars |
| Matrix | Cascade, FadeOut trail, Glitch | Green shades | CyberChars |
| Retro | Scanline, ColorCycle, Glitch(0.02) | Terminal green | Phosphor glow |
| Typewriter | Reveal(LeftToRight), cursor | White | Monospace feel |
| Terminal | Pulse(subtle) | Green | Simple |
| Cinematic | FadeIn(eased), HorizontalGradient, Glow | Gold/White | Drop shadow |
| Elegant | OrganicPulse, Gradient, Shadow | Gold/Cream | Italic option |
| Minimal | FadeIn | Theme default | No decoration |
| Fire | AnimatedGradient(fire), Wave(up) | Fire palette | Bold |
| Ice | Gradient(ice), Sparkle, Pulse | Blue/White | - |
| Hologram | RainbowGradient, Scanline, Pulse | Rainbow | Transparency |

### Builder API:
```rust
// Apply preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)

// Customize after preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)
    .base_color(custom_cyan)  // Override default
    .easing(Easing::Bounce)   // Change animation feel

// Get preset effects for inspection
let effects = TextPreset::Neon.effects();
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_preset_applies_effects: Each preset sets non-empty effect list
- test_preset_customizable: base_color override works
- test_preset_easing_override: Can change easing after preset
- test_all_presets_render: No panic for any preset at t=0, t=0.5, t=1
- test_preset_effects_consistent: Same preset = same effects
- test_preset_no_conflicts: No preset has conflicting effects

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot all 12 presets at t=0.5
- Verify each preset is visually distinct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Preset Library
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

Added StyledText::preset() constructor, preset methods for effects/colors/easing/bold, and comprehensive unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["text-effects"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-12f4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-27kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-2j71","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-3bix","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-3hsz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-3krw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-3r87","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-882b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4r5h","depends_on_id":"bd-atwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4yonz","title":"perf(framebuffer): stride==1 blit full-coverage fast path","description":"Single-lever optimization in DoomFramebuffer::blit_to_painter and QuakeFramebuffer::blit_to_painter: when stride==1 (full coverage), call painter.mark_full_coverage() once and use set_color_at_index_in_bounds for each pixel to avoid per-pixel generation-stamp overhead. Keep sparse stride path using point_colored_at_index_in_bounds. Validate with fmt/check/clippy + framebuffer tests + VFX harness doom/quak.","notes":"Blocked on PurpleCreek exclusive reservations (doom/quak framebuffer + renderers). I validated the current shared-tree diff and posted gates + harness deltas in Agent Mail thread bd-4yonz; ready to land once reservations are released.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:05:03.317001055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:20.227587749Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:20.227567772Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doom","ftui-extras","perf","quake"],"comments":[{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-4yonz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LavenderDesert (2026-02-10): Implemented stride==1 full-coverage fast path in both DoomFramebuffer::blit_to_painter and QuakeFramebuffer::blit_to_painter. Commit c25ab0c6. Validation: 103 doom/quake framebuffer tests pass, golden hash registry (5 scenarios) all pass — output bit-identical. Changes: when stride==1, call painter.mark_full_coverage() then use set_color_at_index_in_bounds (skipping per-pixel generation-stamp overhead). When stride>1, unchanged path using point_colored_at_index_in_bounds.","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:07:49Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-4yonz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LavenderDesert (2026-02-10): Clean-worktree A/B benchmark (commit 8a2bb9e9 vs c25ab0c6, release build, 120x40, 220 frames, seed=42):

Doom E1M1:
  BEFORE total_p95=0.948ms render_p95=0.892ms
  AFTER  total_p95=0.621ms render_p95=0.597ms
  Delta: total -34.49%, render -33.07%

Quake E1M1:
  BEFORE total_p95=1.448ms render_p95=1.427ms
  AFTER  total_p95=1.462ms render_p95=1.438ms
  Delta: total +0.97% (noise), render +0.77% (noise)

The mark_full_coverage + set_color_at_index_in_bounds fast path delivers a substantial 34% win for Doom where blit is a larger fraction of frame time. Quake sees no measurable change because BSP rendering dominates. Golden hash registry passes for all 5 effects.","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6e9","title":"Bubbles Widget Collection (charmed_rust port)","description":"# Bubbles Widget Collection (optional, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks additional widgets inspired by `charmed_rust` (Bubbletea/Bubbles ecosystem).

These are **not required** for the core ftui kernel or the first agent harness deliverable, but we keep them as optional feature-gated extensions.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
The plan explicitly de-scopes “widget zoo” into Phase 7:
- ship kernel + runtime + harness first
- only then add extra widgets

## Scope
Widgets here must:
- render deterministically
- respect focus/input conventions
- avoid terminal side effects (all output goes through ftui’s one-writer routing)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Widgets are feature-gated and cannot affect kernel invariants when disabled.
- [ ] Each widget has snapshot tests + interaction/unit tests where applicable.
- [ ] Widgets integrate with ftui-style/theme and ftui-runtime subscriptions cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:57:47.683736546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867306738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867228872Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":485,"issue_id":"bd-6e9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6e9.1","title":"Implement TextArea widget (multi-line text editor)","description":"# Implement TextArea widget (multi-line text editor)

## Objective
Implement a multi-line text editor widget suitable for agent harnesses and future tools.

This is **not required** for the v1 harness (which needs TextInput), but it’s a valuable Phase 7 extra.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/crates/bubbles/src/textarea.rs`

## Scope
- Editing:
  - insert text (including multi-line paste)
  - backspace/delete
  - newline
  - selection (optional v1; can be phased)
  - undo/redo (optional; can defer)
- Navigation:
  - arrows, home/end
  - word-left/word-right
  - page up/down (optional)
- View:
  - scrolling viewport within the editor
  - cursor visibility and scroll-to-cursor behavior
  - soft-wrap vs hard-wrap policy (explicit)

## Integration Notes
- Reuse text infrastructure rather than inventing a parallel one:
  - Rope storage (`bd-3ky.5`)
  - Editor ops (`bd-3ky.6`)
  - Cursor utilities (`bd-6e9.7`)
  - Grapheme segmentation helpers (`bd-6e9.8`)
- Rendering must be deterministic into `Buffer`/`Frame`.
- All input must come through ftui Event/InputParser.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for editor ops (insert/delete/move) with tricky Unicode.
- Snapshot tests for rendering under:
  - narrow widths
  - long lines
  - multi-line content
  - cursor at boundaries
- PTY test (optional) demonstrating interactive editing without terminal corruption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TextArea supports basic multi-line editing and cursor navigation.
- [ ] Cursor never splits graphemes; visual column math matches ftui width rules.
- [ ] Rendering is stable and scroll-to-cursor works.
- [ ] Unit + snapshot tests cover representative and adversarial cases.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Multi-line text insertion and deletion works correctly
- [ ] Cursor movement: left/right/up/down/home/end/word navigation
- [ ] Text selection with Shift+Arrow, Ctrl+Shift+Arrow, Shift+Home/End
- [ ] Word wrapping: soft wrap preserves buffer, hard wrap inserts newlines
- [ ] Viewport scrolling follows cursor and supports manual scroll
- [ ] Undo/redo: all operations reversible, undo groups by word boundaries
- [ ] Clipboard: copy/cut/paste including bracketed paste support
- [ ] Line numbers display option
- [ ] Placeholder text shown when empty
- [ ] Character limit enforcement prevents exceeding limit
- [ ] Validation callback can reject invalid input

### Unicode Requirements
- [ ] ZWJ sequences (emoji families) treated as single grapheme
- [ ] Wide characters (CJK) render as 2 cells
- [ ] Combining characters handled correctly
- [ ] RTL text renders correctly (if bidi enabled)

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Typing latency < 5ms for buffers up to 100K characters
- [ ] Scrolling latency < 16ms (60 FPS capable)
- [ ] Memory: < 3x document size overhead

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for cursor position changes
- [ ] tracing::debug! for selection changes
- [ ] tracing::trace! for individual character insertions
- [ ] tracing::info! for undo/redo operations
- [ ] tracing::warn! for validation rejections

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for all cursor movement functions
- [ ] Property tests for undo/redo inverse
- [ ] Integration test with real terminal input
- [ ] Snapshot tests for visual output","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:58:07.169424449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:16:38.222416689Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:16:38.222390349Z","close_reason":"TextArea widget implemented with 17 tests, clippy clean, pushed in 5c3afc5","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":486,"issue_id":"bd-6e9.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6e9.2","title":"Implement FilePicker widget","description":"# Implement FilePicker widget (optional)

## Objective
Implement a file picker widget for selecting files/directories inside a TUI.

This is a Phase 7 extra and should be feature-gated.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/crates/bubbles/src/filepicker.rs`

## Scope
- Browse directories
- Filter entries (optional)
- Selection of file or directory (configurable)
- Basic keybindings:
  - up/down
  - enter/open
  - back/go up
  - search (optional)

## Integration Notes
- Must integrate with ftui input/events and rendering model.
- File I/O should be done via async commands/subscriptions where appropriate.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for path navigation logic (pure, no filesystem required using temp dirs or mocked lists).
- Snapshot tests for rendering a small directory listing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FilePicker can navigate up/down, enter directories, and select an entry.
- [ ] Rendering is deterministic and handles long filenames (truncate/ellipsis policy).
